state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (shared equity, open Dec 2025). First Home Super Saver Scheme (FHSS up to $50k). No foreign buyer surcharge waiver.

firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: First Home Buyers Assistance Scheme — full transfer duty exemption (property ≤ $800 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: First Home Buyers Assistance Scheme — concessional transfer duty applies ($800 001–$1 000 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No FHBAS concession (property > $1 000 000). Standard transfer duty applies.

grant.name: NSW FHOG
grant.notes: $10 000 for a newly built or substantially renovated home up to $600,000, or for vacant land plus an eligible building contract up to $750,000. Vacant land eligibility depends on the combined land-and-build cost, so treat the app result as an estimate only.

state.mortgageFeeNotes: Paper fee is $135.80. Electronic mortgage registration and discharge fees are treated here as $125.70 each.
state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. Off-the-plan concession for all buyers until 20 Oct 2026.

firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: First Home Buyer exemption — $0 land transfer duty (property ≤ $600 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: First Home Buyer concession — concessional (sliding scale) duty applies ($600 001–$750 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No First Home Buyer concession (property > $750 000). Standard duty applies.

grant.name: Victorian FHOG
grant.notes: $10 000 for new homes ≤ $750 000. Must never have previously owned residential property in Australia. Occupy as PPR within 12 months for at least 12 continuous months.

state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. QLD Boost to Buy (state shared equity — check qld.gov.au for current status).

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: QLD: established full exemption to $700k, taper to $800k; new build nil duty regardless of value; vacant land exemption under $400k.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: Queensland First Home concession or exemption applies.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: Queensland First Home concession applies on a partial basis.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No Queensland First Home concession applies for this property value or property type.

grant.name: QLD FHOG (Metro)
grant.notes: $15 000 for new homes in Brisbane metro and Peel regions. Extended to 30 June 2026. Property must never have been occupied or sold as a place of residence.

grant.name: QLD FHOG (Regional)
grant.notes: $30 000 for new homes in regional Queensland (outside metro/Peel). Extended to 30 June 2026 as confirmed by QLD Revenue Office in late 2025.

state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. WA has a 7% foreign buyer surcharge.

firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: First Home Owner — full transfer duty exemption (property ≤ $500 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: First Home Owner — concessional transfer duty (sliding scale, $500 001–$600 000).
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No First Home Buyer exemption (property > $600 000). Standard or residential concession duty applies.

grant.name: WA FHOG
grant.notes: $10 000 for new homes south of the 26th parallel (≤ $750 000). $10 000 for new homes north of the 26th parallel (≤ $1 000 000). Only new or substantially renovated homes qualify. Must occupy as PPR within 12 months for at least 6 months.

state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. SA has a 7% foreign buyer surcharge (from 1 Jan 2016).

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: FHB: $0 duty for NEW homes only — no price cap. No concession for established homes.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: SA First Home Buyer stamp duty exemption — $0 duty on new home or vacant land (no price cap, from 6 June 2024).
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: SA First Home Buyer — partial concession does not apply in SA.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: SA provides NO stamp duty concession on established homes. Full duty applies regardless of buyer status.

grant.name: SA FHOG
grant.notes: $15 000 for new homes — NO property value cap (from 6 June 2024). Must never have owned residential property in Australia. Occupy as PPR for at least 6 continuous months commencing within 12 months of settlement. A propertyCap of -1 indicates no cap.

state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. Tasmania has an 8% foreign investor surcharge.

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: FHB: 100% exemption on established homes ≤ $750 000 between 18 Feb 2024 and 30 Jun 2026.
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: Tasmania established-home duty exemption applies: $0 duty for an eligible first home buyer purchasing an established home valued at $750,000 or less, settling between 18 February 2024 and 30 June 2026.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: Tasmania established-home duty relief has no taper. Above $750,000, full standard duty applies.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: No Tasmania established-home duty exemption applies for this property value or date.

grant.name: TAS FHOG ($30k)
grant.notes: $30,000 for eligible new-home transactions commenced between 1 April 2021 and 30 June 2024. Applies to a new home, off-the-plan home, or eligible build transaction. Must satisfy Tasmania First Home Owner Grant eligibility rules.

grant.name: TAS FHOG ($10k transitional)
grant.notes: $10,000 for eligible new-home transactions commenced between 1 July 2024 and 30 June 2025. This transitional period does not require the higher $30,000-specific building criteria.

grant.name: TAS FHOG ($30k current)
grant.notes: $30,000 for eligible new-home transactions commenced between 1 July 2025 and 30 June 2026. Applies to a new home, off-the-plan home, or eligible build transaction and must satisfy Tasmania First Home Owner Grant eligibility rules.

state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. ACT has NO foreign buyer surcharge. Duty paid 14 days after registration (not at settlement).

firstHomeBuyer.summaryLabel: HBCS: $0 duty ≤ $1 020 000 (income test; no property in last 5 yrs); partial concession to $1 455 000
firstHomeBuyer.noteFull: ACT Home Buyer Concession Scheme — $0 conveyance duty (≤ $1 020 000). Income test applies (household taxable income limit); must not have owned property in last 5 years. Check revenue.act.gov.au for current income thresholds.
firstHomeBuyer.noteTaper: ACT Home Buyer Concession Scheme — partial concession applies ($1 020 001–$1 455 000, max saving $35 238). Income and ownership tests apply.
firstHomeBuyer.noteNone: ACT HBCS does not apply (property > $1 455 000 or eligibility criteria not met). Standard conveyance duty payable.

grant.name: ACT HBCS (informational only)
grant.notes: ACT no longer has a cash First Home Owner Grant. The Home Buyer Concession Scheme is already modeled in the duty calculation and should not be added as a separate cash grant.

state.mortgageFeeNotes: Add $69.00 for each additional title on registration or discharge.
state.federalNotes: First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI — uncapped from Oct 2025). Help to Buy (open Dec 2025). FHSS up to $50k. NT has NO foreign buyer surcharge. HomeGrown Territory Grant $50 000 replaces FHOG from Oct 2024.

grant.name: HomeGrown Territory Grant (Established Home)
grant.notes: $10,000 for eligible first home buyers purchasing an established home. This established-home payment ends on 30 September 2025. Occupy as principal place of residence within 12 months and satisfy NT Treasury eligibility rules.

grant.name: HomeGrown Territory Grant (New Home)
grant.notes: $50,000 for eligible first home buyers buying or building a new home in the NT under HomeGrown Territory. Current scheme dates run to 30 September 2026.

grant.name: House and Land Package Exemption
grant.notes: Separate NT house-and-land package exemption that can provide full stamp duty relief for eligible package purchases until 30 June 2027. Check NT Treasury eligibility rules before relying on it.
